(b) GAMELIST/ES_SETTINGS WRITES WITHOUT ES-DE GUARD — enumerated every file touching gamelist.xml/es_settings.xml (17 files) and checked each for a real pgrep/pidof guard (filtering out doc-comment 'ES-DE' false matches). NEW: steam-collection-gen.py (reported). steam-collection-sync.py:83 HAS a proper guard (and a --check bypass) — not a bug. lib/esde_settings.py is read-only (every getter best-effort, no writers). lib/mad_daphne_page.py:399 and lib/madsrv/daphne_cmds.py read the daphne gamelist read-only (explicitly commented 'ES-DE-safe'). clean-manual-cruft.py only READS gamelists (gl_stems). openbor-fetch-media.py only ET.parse-reads GAMELIST. lib/es_collections.py/es_systems.py/gui_theme.py/preview_cmds.py read ES-DE config, no gamelist/es_settings writes.

(c) UNGUARDED regex .group() — grepped all .group( (≈90 hits). Every site I read is guarded by `if not m`/`if m else`, or is inside a re.sub/re.finditer callback where the match object necessarily exists. The only unguarded .group() crashes are already listed ([12.1]/[12.2] steam-fetch-metadata.py:206, [12.5] openbor-fetch-media.py:72). No NEW unguarded-group crash found.

(d) DELETIONS WITHOUT _TMP MOVE — grepped os.remove/unlink/rmtree (Python) and rm (shell). NEW reportable: steam-fetch-media.py:113/134 (additional unlink-before-write sites of [11.3]). Everything else is safe or already listed: steam-fetch-metadata.py:138/145 = [11.4]/[12.3]; mad_backup.py:158 = [6.1]; deck-backup.sh:300 = [6.2]; lib/retroarch_cfg.py:284/348 delete only MAD-auto-managed cfg files (gated by a 'meaningful'/non-empty check, comment 'auto-generated, safe to delete' — MAD-owned, not user data); lib/cemu_cfg.py:143 _clear_port unlinks a managed port file but a backup is taken first (backup_active_once); show-launchscreen.py:57 / router-config-gui.py:92/2449 / sinden_cmds.py:345 are temp/trace files. clean-manual-cruft.py is a GOOD example — it MOVES cruft to /home/deck/Downloads/_TMP with a _MANIFEST.txt, defaults to dry-run, and documents reversibility (no finding). The many `rm -rf "$savesPath/.gaming"` lines in the emulator .sh wrappers are stock EmuDeck cloud-sync cleanup of a transient lock dir (sourced from EmuDeck's all.sh), not user data. scrape-manuals.sh:83/93 `rm -rf "${MEDDIR:?}/$sys"` operate on $TMP=~/.cache/sky-manuals staging dirs (with :? empty-guard), not the live downloaded_media tree.

(e) SUBPROCESS/os.system INJECTION — no os.system anywhere. shell=True only at quit-combo-watcher.py:120 and wiimote-quit-watcher.py:119, both already listed ([10.4]); their quit_cmd/QUITCMD come from policy/env (operator-controlled), not attacker-external input. rpcs3.sh:31 `eval $rpcs3desktopFile` is stock EmuDeck launcher boilerplate operating on a user-supplied .desktop ROM (not MAD-authored) — left out as low-value stock code. All subprocess.run calls with f-strings pass the interpolated value as a list ARGUMENT (no shell), e.g. lib/sinden_cfg.py:171 v4l2-ctl --set-ctrl f'{name}={value}' and the various pgrep -f calls — not injectable.

Confidence: the four reported items are all confirmed by reading the actual code, not just the grep line. Severity is calibrated: only steam-collection-gen.py:171 is high (live-gamelist clobber on the documented run path, matching the listed steam-fetch-metadata findings); the other three are low (narrow truncation windows / in-memory-before-unlink / recoverable calibration data) but are genuine unlisted sites of the project's atomic-write and never-delete rules.

synthesis.risk_summary:
Overall the system is functional and many of its config-writing paths are already careful (the steam-collection-gen sibling steam-fetch-metadata has an ES-DE guard in one place, the router uses a recursive deep_merge, etc.), but the review surfaces one genuinely worrying CLASS of risk that recurs across many independent files: gamelist.xml / cover / emulator-config writers that ignore the project's own hard rules. None of the confirmed findings is rated "critical" after re-verification, but several "high" items can silently destroy user data on completely normal paths.

The sharpest user-facing risks are: (1) steam-fetch-metadata.py [11.4/9.5/12.3] DELETES the user's existing cover art outright (e.link(), no _TMP, no recovery) whenever it cannot fetch a portrait — which includes the everyday case of a network outage — so running metadata fetch offline permanently strips covers; (2) at least five separate scripts (steam-collection-gen [N7.0], steam-fetch-metadata [10.5/9.1], dedup-disc-gamelists [11.1/9.3], skyscraper-apply [11.2/9.4], openbor-gen-gamelist [9.2]) rewrite gamelist.xml with NO check that ES-DE is running — the exact scenario the project rule forbids, where ES-DE rewrites the file on exit and clobbers the script's work (or vice-versa); and (3) model-2-emulator.sh [8.0] has a dead sed targeting the literal string "M2CONFIGFILE", so the lightgun crosshair toggle simply does not work.

Underneath those, there is a consistent pattern of non-atomic config rewrites (cemu/pcsx2/eden/rpcs3/xemu writers, Supermodel.ini, global retroarch.cfg, sinden.conf, calibration JSON) where an interrupted write truncates a live config, plus a few unguarded parses/regex .group() calls that crash a whole game launch or media fetch on a single malformed input. Concurrency issues exist in the long-lived mad-backend daemon (unlocked _ENUM_CACHE mutation, unlocked read-modify-write of shared .cfg files) but are lower-probability. The honest bottom line: this is not a fragile or dangerous codebase day-to-day, but the data-loss findings are real, several trip on ordinary actions, and they violate rules the project itself codified specifically to prevent silent loss — so they deserve fixing before the next big scrape/dedup/restore session.
themes: MISSING ES-DE-RUNNING GUARD on gamelist.xml writers — the single most repeated defect, hitting 5+ independent scripts (N7.0, 10.5, 9.1, 11.1, 9.3, 11.2, 9.4, 9.2). Each rewrites a live gamelist.xml with no check that ES-DE is up, so ES-DE's on-exit rewrite clobbers the change (or the script's write races ES-DE). This is a direct violation of the project's explicit rule.

DELETE-INSTEAD-OF-MOVE / no recoverable _TMP — the never-delete rule is broken in several places: steam-fetch-metadata deletes covers (11.4, 9.5, 12.3), steam-fetch-media unlinks artwork before copying (11.3, N7.2), deck-backup.sh rm-prunes old archives (6.2), mad_backup.reset_local() unlink()s the overrides file (6.1). All destroy user data with no recovery path.

NON-ATOMIC config writes (no temp+rename, often no backup) — pervasive: standalone emulator writers cemu/pcsx2/eden/rpcs3/xemu (5.0, 10.7), Supermodel.ini (8.1), global retroarch.cfg from a game-start path (8.2), sinden.conf (N7.1), gamelist write_text (10.6), and calibration/position JSON across both the madsrv and Tk GUI testers (3.0, N7.3). An interrupted or crashing write truncates the live file.

UNGUARDED PARSE / regex .group() crashes a critical path on one bad input — base controller-policy.toml parse aborts EVERY game launch on a hand-edit typo (0.1, 12.4, 1.5); openbor-fetch-media (12.5), steam-fetch-metadata non-Steam .sh (12.1), and rpcs3 yaml (5.3) crash on malformed input.

RESTORE / RESTORE-SAFETY gaps — do_restore copies emulator configs back with no running-emulator guard (11.5), merges dirs instead of replacing so deleted files resurrect (6.5), and deck-restore.sh extracts over $HOME with no ES-DE-running check (6.6).

LIFECYCLE / CLEANUP leaks in router-config-gui.py — SIGTERM from ES-DE bypasses quit/_clear cleanup entirely (N2.0); quitting orphans ffmpeg preview / leaves Sinden dead (4.0, N0.1); ffmpeg preview never polled so a dead feed claims 'live' and zombies (N1.0); leaked stderr fd per preview (4.2).
INJECTION / metachar handling in regex-replacement templates — device names interpolated unescaped corrupt Cemu XML (12.0, 5.1) and the Sinden config sed (8.8).

DEAD / WRONG GUARD logic — Sinden _mad_sinden nav guard flag is set but never read so aiming drives stray menu nav and trigger-to-quit is dead (N0.0); model-2 crosshair sed targets a literal placeholder string (8.0); X-Arcade escape combo keys off calibration spot state not raw BTN_START (N6.0, 3.3).

CONCURRENCY in the long-lived mad-backend daemon — unlocked _ENUM_CACHE mutation across watch thread + RPC calls (0.2, 10.11) and unlocked read-modify-write of shared .cfg in systems.set_ra_option on the worker pool (2.1).
SYMLINKS in the data layout (against the no-symlinks rule) — install-bezels.sh / install-bezels-all.sh (N3.3) and mugen.sh (8.7) create symlinks into RetroArch overlays and game/data folders.

synthesis.false_positives_note: Wave-1 over-claimed mainly along three lines, and wave-2's executing skeptics correctly cut them. (1) Severity inflation on unreachable mechanisms: [2.0] model2.set raw-resolution corruption and [11.7] reorganize-cd-games no-recovery move were rated medium/data-loss, but the dangerous code path is never reached by the single real caller / the move keeps an undo path — the mechanism existed but the exploit conditions did not, so the rating was unjustified. (2) 'Bug' claims that were actually correct-by-design: [1.1] computing the revert default from the system's own base entry without resolving inherits is intentional; [1.2] the is_int sort never sees a mixed-type list because exactly one caller passes is_int=True with ints; [8.6] supermodel-proton.sh's `&&...||` trap uses the standard guarded idiom and does not skip the reattach; [9.7] the gamelist tag regex only fails on self-closing/attribute tags that ES-DE never emits (verified against MetaData.cpp). (3) Concurrency/leak claims that don't hold once you trace ownership/lifetime: [10.2] devices.watch/unwatch token swap, [10.8] WiiTesterStream slot reader, [10.9] _RUN_ACTIVE lock release, [10.10] X-Arcade zero-node path (it emits a clear 'no_device' end, not a silent no-op), and [4.1] nav-sound zombies (fire-and-forget but not a lifetime leak as claimed). Also [10.4] shell=True quit_cmd is not an injection because the command comes from the user's own trusted TOML. The recurring pattern: wave-1 flagged a real code SHAPE (unlocked global, unescaped string, unguarded parse, delete-before-write) but did not verify REACHABILITY, the actual caller set, or ownership/lifetime — and several of those shapes turned out to be dormant, single-caller-safe, or intentional. Net: the data-loss findings that SURVIVED (gamelist guards, cover deletion, non-atomic writes) are the ones backed by execution and a real everyday trigger, and should be trusted more than the refuted set.
synthesis.coverage_gaps:
Things NOT verified by this review that the user must confirm on-device or that remain open:

ON-DEVICE / HARDWARE (Claude has no display and cannot grab live input):
- All GUI behavior in router-config-gui.py (MAD panel) must be eyeballed: confirm [N0.0] the Sinden lightgun really does drive stray menu nav and that trigger-to-quit is dead; confirm [4.0/N0.1/N1.0] that quitting from the camera page leaves the Sinden dead / ffmpeg orphaned / feed frozen-but-'live'. These were code-confirmed but the on-screen symptom needs a human.
- [8.0] model-2 crosshair: launch bel/gunblade/rchase2 and confirm the crosshair toggle is in fact non-functional (the sed is provably dead, but the in-game effect needs the user).
- [N6.0/3.3] X-Arcade on-cabinet escape combo (P1+P2 Start) after re-calibration — needs the physical X-Arcade.
- [N2.0] SIGTERM-from-ES-DE cleanup gap: confirm whether, after ES-DE kills MAD (not the in-app combo), the Sinden is left dead / LED stuck. Needs a real ES-DE exit.

NOT EXERCISED END-TO-END:
- The actual data-loss on the gamelist writers (N7.0/10.5/11.1/11.2/9.2) was confirmed by STATIC reading of the write + absence of a guard, not by running each script with ES-DE up and watching the clobber. The mechanism is sound and matches the documented ES-DE behavior, but no full clobber was reproduced.
- [11.4/9.5] cover deletion: the unlink path is confirmed, but it was not run against a real Steam fetch with the network down — recommend the user simply not run steam-fetch-metadata offline until fixed.
- mad-backend concurrency ([0.2/10.11/2.1]): confirmed by code (unlocked shared state) but no race was forced under real concurrent RPC load; impact is probabilistic.

DELIBERATELY OUT OF SCOPE / UNREVIEWED:
- The patched ES-DE C++ fork under /home/deck/esde-build/ES-DE was only consulted as a reference (e.g. MetaData.cpp for the gamelist tag shape); it was not itself reviewed for bugs.
- The refuted items ([2.0, 11.7, 1.1, 1.2, 1.4, 4.1, 8.6, 9.7, 10.2, 10.4, 10.8, 10.9, 10.10]) were downgraded/refuted on the specific stated mechanism; a different trigger could in principle revive some, but none is currently a defect.
- No security/permission audit of the Proton wrappers' network or Steam-credential handling beyond the noted shell=True (which traces to trusted local TOML).
- deck-post-update.sh reapply-after-SteamOS-update flow and Samba/udev/systemd persistence were not exercised.
